key_insight: A two-level machine-learning workflow can map phase stability across complex hybrid perovskite alloy compositions by combining fast atomistic relaxations with direct energy prediction.
engineering_relevance: The results identify composition regions with the highest thermodynamic stability, helping engineers prioritize more stable Cs/FA Pb- and Sn-based halide perovskites for solar-cell design.
method: Train graph neural network interatomic potentials on DFT data for efficient structure relaxation.
Use secondary ML models to predict energies directly from unrelaxed alloy structures.
Compute free-energy landscapes across compositions while accounting for alloy disorder and FA molecular orientations.
evidence_signal: The models were evaluated on free-energy landscape calculations for (Cs/FA)Pb(Br/I)3 and (Cs/FA)Sn(Br/I)3, revealing narrower stable regions in the Sn system and the highest stability at high iodine content.
watch_item: Stability predictions depend on the quality and coverage of the DFT training data and may be less reliable in poorly sampled composition or configuration regions.
paper_title: Charting the thermodynamic stability of hybrid perovskite alloys with machine learning
